verses.text: Sins of silence, touching the unclean, or rash oaths bring guilt. Guilt. Many small sins make a man guilty.
Sins of silence, touching the unclean, or rash oaths bring guilt. Guilt. Many small sins make a man guilty.
Sins of silence, touching the unclean, or rash oaths bring guilt. Guilt. Many small sins make a man guilty.
Sins of silence, touching the unclean, or rash oaths bring guilt. Guilt. Many small sins make a man guilty.
He confesses his sin and brings a lamb or kid for a sin offering. Confess. Acknowledged sin is atoned.
He confesses his sin and brings a lamb or kid for a sin offering. Confess. Acknowledged sin is atoned.
The poor may bring two turtledoves instead. The poor. None is shut out for poverty.
The poor may bring two turtledoves instead. The poor. None is shut out for poverty.
The poor may bring two turtledoves instead. The poor. None is shut out for poverty.
The poor may bring two turtledoves instead. The poor. None is shut out for poverty.
The poorest may bring fine flour for a sin offering. Flour. God receives even the humblest gift.
The poorest may bring fine flour for a sin offering. Flour. God receives even the humblest gift.
The poorest may bring fine flour for a sin offering. Flour. God receives even the humblest gift.
A trespass against holy things needs a ram and restitution with a fifth added. Trespass. Wrong done to God is repaid with increase.
A trespass against holy things needs a ram and restitution with a fifth added. Trespass. Wrong done to God is repaid with increase.
A trespass against holy things needs a ram and restitution with a fifth added. Trespass. Wrong done to God is repaid with increase.
Even unknown trespass is guilty and needs a ram. Hidden guilt. Sin is sin, though we know it not.
